description_long: This is a futuristic, military-style airplane with a predominantly green and black color scheme, accented by orange lines and angular, stealth-inspired surfaces. The fuselage is robust and angular, with a cockpit situated at the front and a pair of short, wide wings extending from the midsection, each equipped with engine nacelles. The tail section features a distinctive twin vertical stabilizer configuration, with sharply angled fins and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The side of the fuselage displays the marking 'UEGC' in white, suggesting an organizational or factional affiliation. The airplane's surface appears to be constructed from metal panels with some matte and glossy finishes, and the engines are integrated into the wings rather than mounted below. No visible landing gear is present, and the overall design suggests a focus on speed, maneuverability, and advanced technology.
description: A futuristic, angular green and black airplane with twin vertical stabilizers, mid-mounted wings, and integrated wing engines, marked 'UEGC' on the side.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and wide, angular wings with engine nacelles visible on each side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the 'UEGC' marking, the angular fuselage, and the twin vertical stabilizers at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the engine nacelles under the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the angular body, wing structure, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with short, angular, swept wings featuring integrated engine nacelles and slight anhedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the wings near the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a connecting horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy and angular window design.
